final_verdict.summary: These commands violate every principle of good software design. 80% exceed complexity limits, average 332 lines when 50 would suffice, and cost 35x more tokens than needed. This isn't intelligent automation - it's computational bureaucracy.
final_verdict.recommendation: NUCLEAR OPTION: Delete everything. Start over. Read CLAUDE.md. Apply YAGNI. Ship working solutions instead of architectural monuments.
final_verdict.savage_closing: Your commands have achieved the remarkable feat of making simple tasks complex, efficient operations expensive, and helpful tools overwhelming. Congratulations - you've systematically violated every rule of good software design with mathematical precision. The data doesn't lie: this codebase is a masterclass in how NOT to write automation tools.
